tool.name: ProSeg
tool.description: probabilistic cell segmentation for spatial transcriptomics
tool.command:
## Input preparation ## ProSeg requires the input in the working directory. ## For plain CSV: symlink (no copy, saves disk space). ## For other formats (parquet, tabular): copy the file. ## Galaxy uses ext="csv" for all CSV files regardless ## of compression. ProSeg detects gzip from content.
#if $input_mode.mode == "table" #if $input_mode.transcripts.ext == "csv" ln -s '$input_mode.transcripts' input_data.csv && #else cp '$input_mode.transcripts' input_data.csv && #end if #end if
## ProSeg command
proseg
## Input mode flag #if $input_mode.mode == "anndata" --anndata #elif $input_mode.mode == "zarr" --zarr #end if
## Macro tokens, each block defined in macros.xml @PLATFORM_CMD@ @QV_CMD@ @CELLPOSE_CMD@ @SPACERANGER_CMD@ @MORPHOLOGY_CMD@ @CORE_PARAMS_CMD@ @ADVANCED_CMD@ @OPTIONAL_OUTPUTS_CMD@
## Always write the main SpatialData zarr output --output-spatialdata proseg-output.zarr
## Positional input argument #if $input_mode.mode == "table" input_data.csv #elif $input_mode.mode == "anndata" '$input_mode.anndata_input' #else '$input_mode.zarr_input' #end if
## Error capture 2> proseg_error.log || { cat proseg_error.log; exit 1; }
## Package zarr directory as tar for Galaxy. ## Galaxy cannot pass directories between tools so ## the zarr folder is archived. The downstream EISTA ## tool unpacks this tar before reading. && tar -cf '$output_spatialdata' proseg-output.zarr
## Move optional outputs to Galaxy output paths #if $outputs.counts && mv counts.mtx.gz '$counts' #end if #if $outputs.expected && mv expected.mtx.gz '$expected' #end if #if $outputs.cellmeta && mv cellmeta.csv.gz '$cellmeta' #end if #if $outputs.txmeta && mv txmeta.csv.gz '$txmeta' #end if #if $outputs.gene && mv gene.csv.gz '$gene' #end if #if $outputs.polygons && mv polygons.geojson.gz '$polygons' #end if #if $outputs.polygon_layers && mv polygon_layers.geojson.gz '$polygon_layers' #end if
&& echo "ProSeg run complete." > '$run_summary'

param.name: mode
param.label: Input type
param.help: Select the format of your input data.

param.name: transcripts
param.label: Transcript table
param.help: Table of transcript positions with gene identities and preliminary cell assignments. Accepted formats: CSV, gzipped CSV (.csv.gz), Parquet, tabular.

param.name: anndata_input
param.label: AnnData file (.h5ad)
param.help: AnnData object containing transcript-level spatial data.

param.name: zarr_input
param.label: SpatialData archive (tar)
param.help: SpatialData zarr directory packaged as a tar archive.

param.name: spaceranger_map
param.label: SpaceRanger barcode mappings (VisiumHD only)
param.help: barcode_mappings.parquet from SpaceRanger output. Required when using the VisiumHD platform preset.

tool.help:
ProSeg - Probabilistic Cell Segmentation =========================================
ProSeg segments cells in spatial transcriptomics data by modelling the spatial distribution of transcripts probabilistically using a Cellular Potts Model with MCMC sampling. Unlike image-based segmentation tools, ProSeg uses the transcript positions themselves as the primary signal for boundary determination, making it platform-independent and capable of repositioning transcripts that have diffused away from their cell of origin.
Read the paper: `Jones et al., Nat Methods (2025) <https://doi.org/10.1038/s41592-025-02697-0>`_
-----
Supported platforms --------------------
- **10x Xenium** -- provide the ``transcripts.csv.gz`` or ``transcripts.parquet`` file - **NanoString CosMx** -- provide the ``sample_tx_file.csv`` (pixel or micron coordinates) - **Vizgen MERSCOPE / MERFISH** -- provide ``detected_transcripts.csv.gz`` - **10x Visium HD** -- requires SpaceRanger barcode mappings parquet file - **Manual** -- map your own column names for any platform
-----
Inputs -------
**Transcript table (required)** A table with one row per detected RNA molecule, containing spatial coordinates (x, y, and optionally z) and a preliminary cell assignment from the instrument segmentation software. This preliminary assignment is used to initialise the number and location of cells. ProSeg refines boundaries but does not introduce new cells that were entirely missed by the prior segmentation. Accepted formats: CSV, gzipped CSV (.csv.gz), Parquet, tabular.
**CellPose masks (optional)** A numpy array (.npy) of pixel-level cell assignments from CellPose or similar tools. If provided, ProSeg uses this for a more detailed initialisation. The mask coordinates must be correctly registered to transcript micron coordinates using the scale and affine transform parameters.
-----
Key parameters ---------------
**Voxel size** X/Y resolution of the spatial grid used during sampling. Smaller values give finer boundaries but require more memory. Typical values: 1.0 um for Xenium, 0.5 um for CosMx.
**Burn-in voxel size** Must be an integer multiple of voxel size (e.g. 2.0 when voxel size is 1.0). A coarser resolution is used during the preliminary burn-in phase to reach a good starting configuration efficiently.
**Sampling iterations** 200 is adequate for testing. Use 500 or more for production results. ProSeg is non-deterministic -- results vary slightly between runs due to the MCMC sampling procedure.
**CPU threads** Default 4 is appropriate for shared cluster use. Do not set higher than your Galaxy job allocation.
-----
Outputs --------
**SpatialData archive (always)** A tar archive of a zarr directory readable by the Python ``spatialdata`` package. Contains:
- ``sdata.points["transcripts"]`` -- revised transcript positions - ``sdata.shapes["cell_boundaries"]`` -- cell boundary polygons - ``sdata.tables["table"]`` -- AnnData with count matrix in X, cell metadata in obs, gene metadata in var, centroids in obsm["spatial"]
**Count matrix (optional)** Cell-by-gene integer counts in gzipped Matrix Market format. Read with ``scipy.io.mmread``.
**Cell polygons (optional)** 2D cell boundary polygons in GeoJSON format, flattened from 3D.
-----
Known limitations ------------------
- Cells with very few transcripts will have unreliable boundaries. - ProSeg cannot introduce cells missed entirely by the prior segmentation. - In highly homogeneous regions where neighbouring cells express very similar genes, boundary placement is less reliable. - Results vary slightly between runs due to MCMC non-determinism. - If ProSeg crashes without an error message, it likely ran out of memory. Increase voxel size, reduce voxel layers, or enable Disable diffusion model to reduce memory usage.
